summary: nonenull1 exfiltrates host identity to ngrok tunnel via binding.gyp command expansion
details: The package ships a binding.gyp whose sources field uses GYP command expansion (`<!(node index.js...)`) to execute index.js during `node-gyp rebuild`, which npm auto-runs on any package containing a binding.gyp — even under `--ignore-scripts`. The binding.gyp declares no native sources (no.c/.cc/.cpp files) and its target type is `none`, so its sole purpose is to trigger the embedded command. index.js loads `https` and `os`, collects `os.hostname()`, `os.userInfo()`, and `process.env.GITHUB_REPOSITORY`, and POSTs them to a hardcoded attacker-controlled tunnel at https://crabbing-thong-overhung.ngrok-free.dev. The payload includes a self-identifying field labeled `GYP_BYPASS_IGNORE_SCRIPTS_SUCCESS`, confirming the technique is a deliberate ignore-scripts bypass. Installing this package leaks installer host identity and CI repository context to the operator of the ngrok tunnel.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
